description_long: This object is a cylindrical, metallic can with a visibly weathered and rusted exterior, featuring horizontal ridges running along its body. The can is open on one end, with a detached, circular metal lid lying adjacent to the opening, showing concentric ring patterns typical of tin cans. The interior of the can is visible and appears to be empty, with a slightly lighter metallic finish compared to the exterior. The can's surface displays a mix of brownish rust and faded blue-gray paint, giving it an aged, utilitarian look. The lid is slightly bent, suggesting it was pried open, and there are no visible handles or additional attachments. This type of object is commonly found in urban environments, such as alleys, streets, or near garbage collection points.
description: A weathered, rusted metal can with horizontal ridges and a detached, bent lid, showing signs of age and urban use.
description_view_0: The open end of the can is visible, with the detached lid resting nearby and the interior of the can exposed.
description_view_1: Side view of the can, showing the full cylindrical body with horizontal ridges and the lid lying flat beside the opening.
description_view_2: The closed base of the can is visible, displaying concentric rings and the lid partially visible behind the can.
description_view_3: Side view from the opposite direction, highlighting the can's rusted exterior and the detached lid lying further away.
